 {"id":"gt-975","title":"Molecule execution support for polecats and crew","description":"Enable agents to run bonded molecules through the full lifecycle.

## The Model
1. BOND - bd mol bond <template> --assignee <identity>
   Creates concrete issues, assigns root to agent

2. DISCOVER - Agent finds assigned molecules via bd ready
   DAG structure shows unblocked steps

3. WORK - Agent works through DAG, closing steps as done
   Can delegate children to lower-tier agents (haiku)

4. SURVIVE - Agent dies → beads persist
   Any agent resumes from DAG state

5. SUPERVISE - Witness monitors for stalled molecules
   Nudges owner or reassigns if dead

## Questions to Resolve
- Seed node terminology: seed vs root vs pole vs nucleus
- Assignee inheritance: single owner vs delegation model
- Stall detection: heartbeat vs activity timeout vs session monitoring

## Implementation
- Polecat startup: check for assigned molecules
- Polecat work loop: follow molecule DAG
- Witness: monitor molecule progress, detect stalls
- Mayor: can assign molecules, handle escalations

## Depends On
- beads: bd mol bond command (bd-usro in beads repo)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T16:57:01.09104-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T12:02:25.133021-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T12:02:25.133021-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented molecule execution support for polecats:

1. spawn.go: Added MoleculeContext to work assignment mail with molecule workflow instructions
2. prime.go: Added outputMoleculeContext() that detects molecule steps and shows progress
3. molecule.go: Added 'gt molecule progress' command for Witness monitoring

Polecats now:
- Receive molecule-aware work assignments showing step N/M and workflow
- See molecule context in gt prime output when working on molecule steps
- Can follow the DAG by running 'bd ready --parent <root>' after each step

Witnesses/Mayor can:
- Monitor molecule progress with 'gt molecule progress <root-id>'
- See completion percentage, ready/blocked steps"}

 {"id":"gt-ay1r","title":"gt molecule current: Show what agent should be working on","description":"Query what an agent identity is supposed to be working on via breadcrumb trail.

## Command
```bash
gt molecule current <identity>
gt molecule current gastown/furiosa
gt molecule current deacon
```

## Logic
1. Find handoff bead for identity (pinned bead titled "<role> Handoff")
2. Parse attachment field → molecule ID
3. If no attachment → "naked" (no active molecule)
4. If attached → load molecule, find current step:
   - bd ready --parent=<mol-id> → next unblocked step
   - Or first in_progress step

## Output
```
Identity: gastown/furiosa
Handoff:  gt-8v2 (Furiosa Handoff)
Molecule: gt-mol-abc (mol-polecat-work)
Progress: 3/8 steps complete
Current:  gt-mol-abc.4 - verify-tests
```

Or if naked:
```
Identity: gastown/angharad
Handoff:  gt-9x1 (Angharad Handoff)
Molecule: (none attached)
Status:   naked - awaiting work assignment
```

## Use Cases
- Mayor checking what polecats are doing
- Witness verifying polecat progress
- Debug: "why isnt this polecat working?"
- Deacon patrol: track all agent states","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"gastown/dementus","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:34:01.430109-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T23:43:41.533695-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T23:43:41.533695-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented gt molecule current command"}
-{"id":"gt-az41","title":"mol-ready-work variants: safe vs aggressive","description":"## Summary

Create two variants of mol-ready-work:

### mol-ready-work-safe (default)
- PRs: review and comment, but require human approval for merge
- Issues: triage only, don't auto-close
- Beads: implement but create PR instead of pushing to main

### mol-ready-work-aggressive  
- PRs: can approve and merge directly
- Issues: can close duplicates/invalid
- Beads: can push directly to main

## Implementation
Could be a single molecule with a `{{mode}}` variable, or two separate protos.

 {"id":"gt-b1g","title":"MVP Cutover: GGT replaces PGT for batch work","description":"When this is closed, stop using town and start using gt.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. gt spawn assigns issue to polecat and starts session
2. gt spawn --epic spawns workers for all epic children
3. gt session manages tmux lifecycle  
4. gt send / gt inbox work for mail
5. Refinery processes merge queue with semantic merges
6. Integration branches created and landed correctly
7. gt stop --all halts all sessions
8. One successful test batch completed end-to-end

## What Must Work

- Spawn polecat with issue assignment
- Spawn workers for epic children
- Session start/stop/attach
- Mail send/inbox/read
- Refinery merge loop (semantic)
- Integration branch → main landing
- Witness cleanup protocol
- Emergency stop

## What Can Be Deferred

- Doctor checks (use PGT)
- TUI dashboard
- Plugin system
- Federation
- Ephemeral rigs
- Detailed landing reports

## Test Plan

1. Create epic with 2 tasks, spawn 2 workers
2. Verify polecats get assigned and sessions start
3. Simulate task completion
4. Verify Refinery merges to integration
5. Verify landing to main
6. Verify cleanup

## Validation

Run one real batch implementing GGT issues using GGT.

## Note

No "swarm IDs" - just spawn workers for epic, let merge queue coordinate.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:09.148751-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T03:13:30.970314-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T03:13:30.970314-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:36.196292-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-kmn.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:36.273483-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-kmn.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:36.351097-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-kmn.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:36.431641-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.22","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:36.511124-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-ov2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:51.609649-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-rm3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:51.69062-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T21:36:32.942855-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T21:36:35.053559-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-b2hj","title":"gt orphans: Find lost polecat work","description":"Add a command to find orphaned commits that were never merged to main.

## Problem
Polecat work can get lost when:
- Session killed before merge
- Refinery fails to process
- Network issues during push

## Solution
Add `gt orphans` command that:
1. Uses `git fsck --unreachable` to find dangling commits
2. Filters to recent commits (default: 7 days, configurable)
3. Excludes stash/index entries (WIP on, index on)
4. Shows commit details and suggests recovery

## Usage
```
gt orphans              # Last 7 days
gt orphans --days=14    # Last 2 weeks
gt orphans --recover    # Interactive cherry-pick
```

## Found orphans (Dec 16-20, 2025)
- 3b146c11: Fix mail read auto-ack and add Mayor startup directive
- b6fdc561: Add persistent theme config and fix crew session theming
- 97aba756: Unify gt prime to call bd prime and mail check
- ce769ca5: Add mol-bootstrap molecule for Gas Town installation
- b2219de7: Add bootstrap documentation for new Gas Town installations
- bc82348: feat: Add gt done command (already recovered)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T16:27:25.584819-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T11:11:11.149526-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T11:11:11.149526-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented gt orphans command"}

 {"id":"gt-drbd","title":"Add no-PR instructions to mol-polecat-work at two points","description":"Update mol-polecat-work in builtin_molecules.go to explicitly forbid GitHub PRs.

## Two Points to Add Instructions

### 1. submit-work step
When polecat is ready to submit:
- Push branch to origin
- Create beads merge-request issue
- DO NOT use gh pr create or GitHub PRs

### 2. CLAUDE.md polecat context
Add to polecat role instructions:
- Never use gh pr create
- Never create GitHub pull requests
- The Refinery processes merges via beads MR issues

## Why Two Points
- Molecule step description guides the workflow
- CLAUDE.md reinforces at context level
- Belt and suspenders approach

## Implementation
1. Update PolecatWorkMolecule() submit-work step description
2. Update prompts/roles/polecat.md with explicit prohibition

Related: gt-44wh (general no-PR bug)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:44:51.497283-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T15:06:02.248711-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T15:06:02.248711-08:00","close_reason":"Added no-PR instructions to mol-polecat-work submit-merge step and polecat.md role docs","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-drbd","depends_on_id":"gt-44wh","type":"related","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:44:57.503314-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"gt-drp5","title":"mol-ready-work: graceful GitHub/label handling","description":"## Summary

mol-ready-work assumes:
- GitHub repo exists with gh CLI configured
- Labels 'untriaged' and 'triaged' exist

Should handle gracefully:
1. No GitHub repo (beads-only project) → skip GH backlogs
2. Missing labels → skip that backlog tier, don't error
3. gh CLI not authenticated → warn and skip GH backlogs

## Implementation
Add checks in scan-backlogs step:
```bash
# Check if gh is available and authenticated
if gh auth status &>/dev/null; then
  # scan GH backlogs
fi
```

 {"id":"gt-dt5","title":"Define Engineer as the Refinery agent role","description":"Clarify the distinction:

- **Refinery** = place/module/directory/workspace
  - `<rig>/refinery/` directory structure
  - `gt refinery start/stop/status` commands
  - tmux session name: `refinery` or `<rig>-refinery`

- **Engineer** = role/agent who works in the Refinery
  - CLAUDE.md prompting: "You are an Engineer..."
  - Documentation: "The Engineer processes merge requests..."
  - Mail address: `<rig>/engineer` (or `<rig>/refinery`?)

Updates needed:
- Add Engineer role description to docs
- Update CLAUDE.md templates for refinery agents
- Keep `gt refinery` commands as-is (they manage the place)
- Internal code stays `internal/refinery/` (the module)

Future consideration: Multiple Engineers in one Refinery for parallel merge processing.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T23:02:18.591842-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T23:07:21.93783-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-dt5","depends_on_id":"gt-h5n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T23:02:55.577196-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

On Witness session start:

## Desired behavior
On Witness session start:
1. gt prime detects RoleWitness

 {"id":"gt-mzal.7","title":"Proto marketplace: shareable molecule templates","description":"Enable sharing protos between Gas Town installations.

## Vision

A public registry of protos that users can pull and use:

```bash
gt proto search "code review"
gt proto install gastown/code-review
gt sling code-review gastown/Toast --wisp
```

## Registry Design

### Local Catalog
`~/gt/molecules/` - user-defined and installed protos

### Remote Registry
`registry.gastown.dev/protos/` (future)
- Browse online catalog
- Version-controlled protos
- Rating/reviews
- Usage statistics

## Proto Package Format

```
gastown-code-review-1.0.0/
├── PROTO.md          # Main proto definition
├── README.md         # Usage documentation
├── LICENSE           # Usage terms
└── plugins/          # For pluggable molecules
    ├── security/
    └── performance/
```

## Commands

```bash
gt proto list              # Show installed protos
gt proto search <query>    # Search registry
gt proto install <name>    # Install from registry
gt proto publish <path>    # Publish to registry
gt proto update            # Update all installed
```

## For Now

Start with local catalog. Marketplace is future phase.
Ensure proto format is registry-compatible from the start.
","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T21:00:49.124222-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T21:00:49.124222-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-mzal.7","depends_on_id":"gt-mzal","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-22T21:00:49.124687-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-mzal.7","depends_on_id":"gt-mzal.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T21:01:01.874557-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-mzal.8","title":"Update Mayor startup protocol for bootstrap","description":"Teach Mayor to respond to "boot up gas town" by slinging mol-gastown-boot.

## Current Protocol

1. Announce: "Mayor, checking in."
2. Check mail
3. If handoff, continue
4. Await user instruction

## Enhanced Protocol

1. Announce: "Mayor, checking in."
2. Check mail
3. If handoff, continue
4. **If user says "boot"/"startup"/"bootstrap":**
   - Sling mol-gastown-boot as wisp
   - Execute verification-gated steps
   - Report town status when complete
5. Otherwise await instruction

## Trigger Phrases

- "boot up gas town"
- "bootstrap the town"
- "start gas town"
- "bring up the town"

## Execution

Mayor runs the molecule manually (not via subagent):

```
1. Bond the proto: bd mol bond mol-gastown-boot --wisp
2. For each step:
   a. Run action command
   b. Loop verification with backoff
   c. On stall, run recovery
   d. Close step when verified
3. Squash wisp with summary
4. Report: "Gas Town is up. All agents healthy."
```

## CLAUDE.md Update

Add to Mayor CLAUDE.md:

```markdown
## Bootstrap Command

When user requests town bootstrap:
1. gt sling gastown-boot mayor/ --wisp
2. Execute molecule steps with verification
3. Keep trying until all agents healthy
4. No timeouts - you are the town engineer
```

 {"id":"gt-n7z7","title":"Bug: refinery --foreground detects parent session as already running","description":"When gt refinery start gastown runs:
1. Creates tmux session gt-gastown-refinery
2. Sends 'gt refinery start gastown --foreground' into the session
3. The foreground command checks HasSession() - finds the session it's inside
4. Returns 'already running' error

The foreground mode check should either:
- Skip the tmux session check (only check PID)
- Use a different indicator that the daemon loop is running
- Pass a flag to indicate we're being called from the background starter

Workaround: Manually run 'gt refinery start gastown --foreground' from a fresh session.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:56:20.326369-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T03:52:11.20518-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T03:52:11.20518-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-nam3","title":"Update docs to reflect molecule-first paradigm","description":"Gas Town is fundamentally a molecule execution engine. Documentation should reflect this more clearly.

## Issues Found

### 1. gt spawn examples show molecule as optional
README.md line 116: `gt spawn --issue <id> # Start polecat on issue`
Should emphasize: polecats execute molecules, not just issues.

### 2. Architecture.md spawn examples inconsistent
Line 344 shows molecule: `gt spawn --issue gt-xyz --molecule mol-engineer-in-box`
Line 1434 shows without: `gt spawn --issue <id>`

### 3. Config vs molecule distinction not clear
outposts.yaml shows static policy - should note when molecules apply.

### 4. Operational molecules section is good but buried
Lines 430-566 cover operational molecules well. Should be more prominent.

## Updates Needed
- [ ] README: Update spawn examples to show molecule usage
- [ ] architecture.md: Ensure all spawn examples include molecules
- [ ] architecture.md: Add section on "when config vs molecule"
- [ ] architecture.md: Move operational molecules higher in document
- [ ] Add principle: "If it requires cognition, it's a molecule"
- [ ] federation-design.md: Note that policy can escalate to mol-outpost-assign

## Key Message
Gas Town doesn't spawn workers on issues. It spawns workers on molecules.
The issue is just the seed data for the molecule execution.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:26:31.842406-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T09:26:21.813833-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T09:26:21.813833-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-qao","title":"CLI: mayor commands (start, attach, stop, status)","description":"Mayor management CLI commands.

## Commands

### gt mayor start
```
gt mayor start [--continue] [--agent AGENT]
```
- --continue: Resume from previous session (check for handoff mail)
- --agent: claude (default) or other agent type

### gt mayor attach
```
gt mayor attach
```
Attach to running Mayor session.

### gt mayor stop
```
gt mayor stop [--grace-period N]
```
Stop Mayor session with optional grace period.

### gt mayor status
```
gt mayor status [--json]
```
Show Mayor running status.

## Session Management
Session name: `gt-mayor`
Working directory: `<town>/mayor/rig/` or `<town>/mayor/`

## Implementation
Similar to session commands but for special Mayor context.

```go
func getMayorPath(townRoot string) string {
    return filepath.Join(townRoot, "mayor")
}

func getMayorSessionName() string {
    return "gt-mayor"
}
```

## New File
internal/cmd/mayor.go

## PGT Reference
gastown-py/src/gastown/cli/mayor_cmd.py

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] gt mayor start launches Mayor session
- [ ] gt mayor attach works
- [ ] gt mayor stop with grace period
- [ ] gt mayor status shows running state","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:47:54.721035-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:05:45.226324-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-qh2","title":"Session cycling UX: smooth transitions via TUI wrapper","description":"## Problem

Current CLI agent session cycling is painful:
- Shell → CC starts → priming → context loads → ready → work → exit/crash → repeat
- Each cycle is 30-60 seconds of cold boot
- No continuity between shell and agent's inner state
- Raw "session not running, starting..." loop is the baseline

## GGT Advantages (already have)

- Beads: Work state survives session death completely
- Mail: Handoff notes from past-self to future-self  
- Prime commands: Structured context reload

## Gap: Transition Mechanics

Ideas to explore when actively using CLI:

1. **In-band cycling** - `/restart` or `/cycle` command, agent handles own restart without dropping to shell

2. **Hot standby** - TUI maintains pre-warmed session in background, switch to already-primed agent

3. **Persistent wrapper** - Bubbletea TUI stays running across session cycles, CC sessions come/go inside it

4. **Session pooling** - Keep 2-3 primed sessions ready, never wait for cold start

## Deferred

Deliberately P4 until we're actively using the simpler CLI and feel the pain firsthand.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:38:12.660716-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T23:17:34.27061-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-qivm","title":"gt crew at: auto-prime when exec'ing Claude in-session","description":"When running 'gt crew at <name>' from inside the target session, we exec Claude directly. But this means we can't send 'gt prime' afterward since we ARE the process.

Possible solutions:
1. Claude startup hook that runs gt prime
2. Pass prompt as argument to claude CLI
3. Wrapper script approach

Related: crew resume prompt also can't be sent in this path.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T15:13:38.035775-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T11:50:55.924767-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T11:50:55.924767-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented: execClaude now passes 'gt prime' as initial prompt to Claude CLI"}

 {"id":"gt-s18k","title":"Witness patrol: stale MR queue detection needs state tracking","description":"The mol-witness-patrol check-refinery step says 'if queue is non-empty for >30 min, send nudge' but doesn't explain how to track when the queue first became non-empty.

Without this, the Witness can't detect stale queues.

 {"id":"gt-s3m0","title":"gt polecat: add 'done' or 'finish' command to transition from working to idle","description":"When a polecat finishes work but session wasn't properly cleaned up, there's no way to reset it from 'working' state back to 'idle'.

Tried:
```
gt polecat sleep gastown/Angharad
Error: sleeping polecat: polecat is not active (state: working)
```

The sleep command only works on 'active' polecats, not 'working' ones.

Had to manually edit state.json to reset:
```
jq '.state = "idle" | .issue = ""' state.json
```

Need a command like:
```
gt polecat done gastown/Angharad   # working -> idle
gt polecat finish gastown/Angharad # working -> idle  
gt polecat reset gastown/Angharad  # any state -> idle (force)
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-19T01:41:39.851037-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T01:57:17.031611-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T01:57:17.031611-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-sye","title":"Mayor startup protocol prompting","description":"Add startup protocol to Mayor CLAUDE.md template.

## On Session Start

1. Check for handoff:
   town inbox | grep "Session Handoff"

2. If handoff found:
   - Read it: town read <msg-id>
   - Process pending escalations (highest priority)
   - Check status of noted swarms
   - Verify rig health matches notes
   - Continue with documented next steps

3. If no handoff:
   town status     # Overall health
   town rigs       # Each rig
   bd ready        # Work items
   town inbox      # Any messages
   Build your own picture of current state.

4. After processing handoff:
   - Archive or delete the handoff message
   - You now own the current state

## Handoff Best Practices

- Be specific: 'Toast has merge conflict in auth/middleware.go' not 'Toast is stuck'
- Include context: Why decisions are pending, what you were thinking
- Prioritize next steps: What is most urgent
- Note time-sensitive items: Anything that might have changed since handoff","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:15:27.915484-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T20:48:57.555724-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-sye","depends_on_id":"gt-u82","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:15:39.459108-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-szsq","title":"gt spawn --create should auto-add polecat if missing","description":"## Problem

`gt spawn gastown --issue gt-xxx --create` fails if no polecats exist.

## Current Behavior

```
Error: auto-select polecat: no available polecats in rig 'gastown'
```

## Expected Behavior

`--create` should:
1. Create a new polecat if none exist
2. Or create the specified polecat if `gt spawn gastown/NewName --create`

## Workaround

Must manually run `gt polecat add gastown Name` first.

## Principle

Agent UX should be forgiving - if I say spawn with --create, make it work.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-18T21:52:07.75062-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T01:33:49.859926-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T01:33:49.859926-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-tca","title":"Polecats should auto-cleanup after MR submission","description":"Currently polecats must manually run 'gt handoff --shutdown' after completing work. This is error-prone and leaves stale polecats around.

## Desired Flow

1. Polecat completes work
2. Polecat runs 'gt mq submit' (or similar)
3. MR is added to integration queue
4. **Polecat automatically cleans up** (no manual handoff needed)

## Implementation Options

### Option A: mq submit triggers cleanup
In 'gt mq submit':
1. Submit MR to queue
2. Automatically run cleanup (same as gt handoff --shutdown)
3. Polecat session terminates

### Option B: Refinery triggers cleanup
When Refinery picks up MR:
1. Refinery processes MR
2. Sends message to Witness: 'CLEANUP: <polecat>'
3. Witness cleans up polecat

### Option C: Molecule-driven
Define cleanup as final phase of polecat-work molecule:
1. code → test → submit-mr → cleanup

## Note
This reinforces the ephemeral model: polecats exist only for the duration of a single task.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T15:22:54.485456-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T11:44:14.020856-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T11:44:14.020856-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented: mq submit now auto-cleanups polecats after MR submission","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-tca","depends_on_id":"gt-9nf","type":"related","created_at":"2025-12-20T15:40:08.998908-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
